verification_gate: Each migration wave has bounded source and target paths, adapter and native stages, baseline and parity commands, data migration, rollback, proof, and no-loss acceptance.

clause_every_wave_has_bounded_source_pat.evidence: Each wave names verbatim peer source paths from generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv, e.g. W1 crates/codedb_core (BlobStore) + crates/codedb_core/capture_policy.rs; W3 crates/codedb_store_redb (1761 ln, 14 tests); W4 crates/codedb_store_pg (1281 ln); W7 envctl crates/engine (DbSnapshot, migration_db, catalog); W8 crates/codedb_mcp (1764 ln) + envctl crates/{cli,gui,engine} db*.rs; W9 crates/codedb_rust_static + crates/codedb_build_capture (5503 ln); W10 envctl crates/ libSQL/RuVector/semantic/secrets.

clause_every_wave_has_bounded_target_pat.evidence: Each wave names LifeOS bounded-context targets from the target_lifeos_owner column, e.g. lifeos codedb store, lifeos secret boundary, lifeos codedb ingestion, lifeos postgres store (POSTGRES-004/005), lifeos codedb materialization, lifeos envctl bridge, lifeos migration engine, lifeos catalog, lifeos codedb MCP surface, lifeos envctl-db surface, lifeos codedb compiler lane, lifeos codedb build broker, lifeos external store (subordinate), lifeos postgres+ruvector (POSTGRES-005), lifeos intelligence substrate (INTEL-*), lifeos secret authority (external).

clause_every_wave_has_adapter_and_native.evidence: All 10 waves carry both an adapter_stage (land the peer capability behind a LifeOS anti-corruption port) and a native_stage (re-home as native LifeOS code). Two-stage adapter->native structure is present for every wave including W5 (dual-write window -> PostgreSQL sole writer) and W10 (disarmed port -> gated activation).

clause_every_wave_has_baseline_and_parit.evidence: Each wave carries baseline_commands at peer HEAD and parity_commands against the LifeOS-homed code, grounded in the ci_receipt and parity_gate columns, e.g. W1 blobstore_parity.rs (13) / test_plugin_secret_guard.nu; W3 test_redb_lifecycle.nu (14); W4 CDB106 PG17 (13) / test_mandatory_pg_parity.py; W5 live blobstore_parity.rs vs disposable PostgreSQL; W6 materialization_paths.rs (7) / CDB035; W8 policy_and_stdio.rs (24) / db_parity.rs; W9 compiler_observed_tests.rs (2/2) / capture_approved_fixture_build.

clause_every_wave_has_data_migration.evidence: Each wave specifies its data-migration handling with a concrete value. Nine waves specify 'none / abstraction / read-only / derived / carried-schema-versioned-backup' with the reason; exactly one wave (W5) specifies the real durable move: forward-copy every redb-durable source-blob into PostgreSQL, verify SHA-256 per blob, flip the durable writer only after the full set matches.

clause_every_wave_has_rollback.evidence: Each wave carries a rollback that keeps peers and the application unchanged (revert the LifeOS-homed module; peer crate untouched). W5's rollback is the load-bearing one: redb-durable is retained until PostgreSQL parity is green; on any SHA-256 mismatch the PostgreSQL writer is disarmed and redb remains the durable authority; no blob is deleted during cutover.

clause_every_wave_has_proof.evidence: Each wave names a planned per-wave proof (proof_records/CONSOLIDATE-002-W{1..10}) tied to the green parity command(s) for that wave. W5 and W10 additionally record the STORE-001 open-dependency proofs (live blobstore_parity.rs vs disposable PostgreSQL; live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation; secretd runbook).

clause_every_wave_has_no_loss_acceptance.evidence: Each wave carries a no_loss_acceptance clause that preserves behavior, history, tests, and provenance, and carries forward the exact known_gap from the ADOPT map rather than dropping it (e.g. W4 dual-backend receipt absent; W6 multi-OS Linux-fallback; W8 symbols/deploy/watch unwired; W9 FAKE rustc / production broker unbuilt). W5 asserts every redb-durable blob maps to exactly one identical-SHA-256 PostgreSQL row with zero unmatched and redb retained (not deleted).

clause_dependency_ordered.evidence: Every wave carries depends_on edges over the BlobStore seam; the linear execution_order [W1..W10] is a valid topological sort (W1 has no deps; W2->W1; W3->W1,W2; W4->W1,W3; W5->W3,W4; W6->W4,W5; W7->W4; W8->W5,W6; W9->W2,W4; W10->W4,W5 + external gates). No cycles.

capability_coverage.governing_row_excluded_from_waves: Final store authority (STORE-001) is the settled decision that governs the waves, not a capability that migrates; it is referenced as governing_decision, not placed in a wave.

store_001_alignment.durable_class_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (W4 lands PgStore; W5 makes it sole durable writer)
store_001_alignment.transient_class_owner: redb (W3 lands it as transient; W5 demotes durable-on-redb to transient forward buffer after verified parity)
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover_invariant_operationaliz: W5 (live blobstore_parity.rs vs disposable PostgreSQL; class leaves redb-durable only after green)
store_001_alignment.no_unverified_cutover_for_semantic_and_e: W10 holds RuVector/semantic/libSQL/secrets behind disarmed ports until live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation, operator unfreeze, and secretd runbook clear

input_grounding.substitute_source_used: generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv (Complete ADOPT-001/ADOPT-002 capability map) + CONSOLIDATE-001 task_graph row (receiving bounded contexts)
input_grounding.note: CONSOLIDATE-001's capability_merge_matrix.json is not yet materialized; every wave is grounded verbatim in the adopted-capabilities map (source paths, CI receipts, parity gates, boundaries, known gaps, target owners) and STORE-001, not invented.

proof_summary: CONSOLIDATE-002 defines ten dependency-ordered, upgrade-only migration waves in generated/task_families/CONSOLIDATE-002.json that physically merge all 18 preserved peer code capabilities (nu_plugin CodeDB lane + envctl bridge/engine lane, from generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv) into the single LifeOS Rust application, plus the one verified STORE-001 durable cutover. The ordering is topological over the BlobStore seam: W1 lands the content-addressed BlobStore trait and secret-output guards as the anti-corruption port; W2 lands SHA-256 workspace identity + resumable capture; W3 lands the redb source-blob store as the transient tier; W4 lands PgStore as the durable backend; W5 executes the single verified durable cutover (forward-copy every redb-durable blob into PostgreSQL, verify SHA-256 per blob against a disposable PostgreSQL via blobstore_parity.rs, then flip PostgreSQL to sole durable writer and demote redb to transient buffer); W6 lands exact-byte materialization + the envctl export contract as the derived-projection path; W7 lands the replayable migration engine + catalog; W8 lands the read-only bounded MCP + envctl db code-graph surfaces; W9 lands the two EXTEND-class capabilities (compiler-observed evidence, sandboxed build capture) with their FAKE-rustc / unbuilt-broker gaps carried forward as tracked child work; W10 holds the deferred/externally-gated classes (remote libSQL store subordinate, PostgreSQL-RuVector component, semantic consumer, external secrets engine) behind disarmed ports until live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation, operator unfreeze of meta-ruvector, and the secretd runbook clear. Every wave carries all ten gate fields with concrete, source-cited values (bounded source path, bounded target path, adapter stage, native stage, baseline command, parity command, data migration, rollback, proof, no-loss acceptance), and each capability appears in exactly one wave with no second implementation wave. The store roles are not re-litigated: they are governed by the settled STORE-001 decision (PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical durable owner; redb transient tier; envctl forward-only bridge/projection; AgentDB non-authoritative cognition), and the single-durable-writer, forward-only-sync, and verified-cutover invariants are honored. CONSOLIDATE-001's capability_merge_matrix.json is not yet on disk, so the waves are grounded in the Complete ADOPT-001/ADOPT-002 adopted-capabilities map and the CONSOLIDATE-001 target bounded contexts rather than invented. No product code, schema, database, or peer mutation was performed; this is a planning artifact only.
inputs_grounded_in: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
